meta.title: ppush — secure secret sharing
meta.description: Share passwords, confidential text and files through end-to-end encrypted, self-destructing links.
meta.homeDescription: Share a password, text or file as an end-to-end encrypted, self-destructing link. Zero-knowledge: the server never sees your secret, no account required.
meta.aboutDescription: How ppush works: zero-knowledge encryption in your browser, self-destructing links, view limits and tracking. Data hosted in France, delivered via Cloudflare.
meta.apiDocsDescription: ppush REST API: create and manage end-to-end encrypted, self-destructing secret links programmatically using personal access tokens.
meta.legalDescription: Legal notice for ppush: publisher, hosting, GDPR and LCEN compliance, data retention and contact for the secure secret-sharing service.
meta.privacyDescription: ppush privacy policy: data processed, legal bases, retention periods and your rights. Secrets are end-to-end encrypted and never readable by the server.
meta.loginDescription: Sign in to ppush to access your secret history, view tracking and larger file uploads. Passwordless sign-in with a passkey is supported.
meta.registerDescription: Create a free ppush account to see who opened your secrets, keep a history of your links, share larger files and enjoy longer retention.

footer.tagline: End-to-end encrypted, by you. Created and maintained by Alexandre.

home.title: Share a secret
home.subtitle: An end-to-end encrypted link that self-destructs. Nothing transits or is stored in cleartext.
home.anonLimits: Limits: up to {days} days · {views} views · {fileMb} MB max per file
home.anonUnlock: Create a free account to unlock:
home.anonPerkTrackingDesc: Track your pushes — get date, time, IP and browser of recipients
home.anonPerkHistoryDesc: View and manage the links you've generated
home.anonPerkMoreDesc: Files up to {fileMb} MB, links up to {days} days and {views} views.
home.anonCtaNote: No password needed — a passkey is enough.

form.textPlaceholder: Paste the sensitive content to share here…
form.urlHint: The recipient will be redirected after decryption.
form.passphraseHint: The recipient will have to enter it before accessing the secret.
form.retrievalHint: Protects against automatic previews (messaging apps, antivirus) that would burn a view.
form.deletableHint: Shows a “destroy this secret” button on the view page.
form.noteHint: Visible only to you, in your history. Never encrypted — do not put anything sensitive in it.
form.footnote: Encrypted in your browser — the server never sees the content or the key.

generator.ambiguousHint: l, I, O, 0, 1 — excluded by default to avoid misreadings.
generator.guarantee: Each enabled type is guaranteed to appear at least once. Entropy: ~{bits} bits.

fileDrop.prompt: Drag a file here or click to browse
fileDrop.limit: Up to {mb} MB — encrypted before upload
fileDrop.freeSpace: · {space} of free space on the service
fileDrop.quotaAlmostFull: Personal quota almost reached: {left} remaining — space frees up when your files expire, or by expiring them from your history.
fileDrop.almostFull: Storage almost full: {left} currently available (free service — space frees up as existing files expire).
fileDrop.tooBig: This file exceeds the space currently available on the service ({max}).

success.title: Secret link created
success.expiry: Expires after {delay} or {views, plural, one {# view} other {# views}}
success.qrAlt: QR code of the secret link
success.warning: ⚠️ The decryption key lives in the link fragment (after the #): it was never sent to the server. Without the full link, the secret is unrecoverable — including by us.
success.anonTip: Tip: with a free account, you keep this link in your history and track who opened it, and when.
success.recipientHint: Read-only — copy it and send it along with the link.
success.recipientNotice: Heads-up: this link is valid for {delay} or until it has been opened {views, plural, one {# time} other {# times}} — whichever comes first. After that it self-destructs and the secret is permanently lost.

viewer.expiredTitle: This secret is no longer available
viewer.expiredBody: It expired, reached its maximum number of views, or was deleted. The content has been permanently erased from the server.
viewer.burnedBody: The content has been permanently deleted from the server.
viewer.noKeyBody: The decryption key (the part after the #) is missing or invalid. Without it, the secret cannot be decrypted — not even by the server. Ask the sender to resend the full link.
viewer.gateTitle: A secret has been shared with you
viewer.gatePassphrase: Enter the passphrase you were given to unlock it.
viewer.gateReveal: Click below to reveal it. This view will be counted.
viewer.decryptFailed: Decryption failed: the link is incomplete or corrupted. Make sure you copied the entire URL (including the part after the #).
viewer.revealedNote: Keep it somewhere safe: this link may not be viewable again.
viewer.downloadFailed: Failed to download or decrypt the file.
viewer.burnConfirm: Permanently destroy this secret? This cannot be undone.
viewer.burnAction: I have retrieved the secret — destroy it now
viewer.pageFootnote: End-to-end encrypted · the server cannot read this secret
viewer.thanksText: ppush handed it to you, end-to-end encrypted. Want to send your own secret, just as safely?
viewer.countdownHint: Open it soon — it won't wait.
viewer.expiredInviteTitle: Need to share a secret of your own?
viewer.expiredInviteText: ppush creates end-to-end encrypted links that self-destruct after a delay or a few views. Free, no account needed.
viewer.safetyCaution: If you didn't expect to land on this page, or you're not expecting anyone to send you information: don't reveal the secret. It may not be meant for you, or it could have been sent to deceive you — we have no way of knowing what it contains.
viewer.safetyTrust: Conversely, if this link was given to you by a person or service you trust, it most likely holds something useful — retrieve it before the link expires.

pushes.title: History
pushes.empty: {filter, select, active {No active pushes for now.} expired {No expired pushes for now.} other {No pushes for now.}}

pushDetail.keyNote: ℹ️ The server does not know the decryption key: the full URL (with the part after the #) only exists where you copied it at creation.
pushDetail.expireConfirm: Expire this push immediately? The content will be permanently purged.

account.title: Account
account.defaultDeletable: Destruction by the recipient by default
account.newPasswordHint: At least 12 characters. Other sessions will be signed out.
account.setPasswordHint: Optional — lets you also sign in with a password (you currently use a passkey).
account.passkeysIntro: Sign in without a password — phishing-resistant. Add the devices you trust.
account.passkeyError: Passkey operation failed or was cancelled.
account.passkeyUnsupported: Your browser does not support passkeys.
account.deleteAccountText: Erases your account and all your data — pushes, sessions, passkeys, API tokens — immediately and permanently. This cannot be undone. You can create a fresh account afterwards.
account.deleteAccountConfirm: Type your email address to confirm
account.deleteAccountFinal: This permanently erases your account and all your data. Continue?
account.deleteReauthHint: For security, confirm with your credentials.

tokens.title: API tokens
tokens.intro: To create pushes from scripts: Authorization: Bearer ‹token› on the /api/pushes API. See the README for the CLI client that encrypts locally.
tokens.created: Token created — copy it now, it will never be shown again:
tokens.revokeConfirm: Revoke this token? Integrations using it will stop working.

totp.title: Two-factor authentication (2FA)
totp.intro: A 6-digit TOTP code is required at every sign-in (Google Authenticator, 2FAS, Bitwarden…). If you lose your device, an administrator can reset 2FA.
totp.scan: Scan with your app, or enter the key:

admin.title: Administration
admin.resetTotpConfirm: Reset 2FA for {email}? It will have to be set up again.
admin.deleteConfirm: Permanently delete {email} and all their data (pushes, sessions, passkeys, tokens)? This cannot be undone.

auth.registerSubtitle: Free — an email address and a password are all you need.
auth.name: Name
auth.pendingBody: Your account is awaiting approval by an administrator. You will be able to sign in as soon as it is activated.
auth.totpSubtitle: Enter the code from your authenticator app.
auth.passkeyError: Passkey sign-in failed or was cancelled.
auth.passkeyUnsupported: Your browser does not support passkeys.
auth.passkeyHero: No password to remember — use Face ID, your fingerprint or a security key. Faster, and impossible to phish.
auth.perk1: Keep a history of every link you create
auth.perk2: See who opened your secret, and when
auth.perk3: Bigger files, longer expiry, more views
auth.perksFree: Free, forever. No ads, no tracking.

api.tooManyRequests: Too many requests, try again in a minute
api.loginTooMany: Too many attempts, try again later
api.accountPending: Account awaiting approval by an administrator
api.captchaFailed: Anti-bot check failed — please try again.
api.passkeyExpired: Passkey challenge expired — please try again.
api.passkeyLastMethod: Set a password before removing your last passkey, or you would be locked out.
api.deleteConfirmMismatch: The email address does not match.
api.deleteLastAdmin: You are the only administrator — promote another admin before deleting your account.
api.cannotDeletePrimaryAdmin: The primary administrator account cannot be deleted.
api.emailTaken: An account already exists with this email
api.passwordMin: Password must be at least 12 characters
api.newPasswordMin: The new password must be at least 12 characters
api.passwordTooMany: Too many attempts, try again in 15 minutes
api.pushAnonLimit: Too many links created without an account. Try again later or create a free account.
api.pushUserLimit: Too many links created. Try again in an hour.
api.maxDays: Maximum duration: {days} days{anon, select, true { without an account (create a free account for more)} other {}}
api.maxViews: Maximum views: {views}{anon, select, true { without an account (create a free account for more)} other {}}
api.blobRequired: blobPath is required for a file
api.blobMissing: Blob not found — upload the file first
api.fileTooBigMax: File too large (max {mb} MB{anon, select, true { without an account (create a free account for more)} other {}})
api.blobAnonLimit: Too many uploads without an account. Try again later or create a free account.
api.blobUserLimit: Too many file uploads. Try again in an hour.
api.linkNotFound: This link does not exist or has been deleted
api.secretExpired: This secret has expired and is no longer available
api.burnNotAllowed: Destruction by the recipient is not allowed
api.tooManyAttempts: Too many attempts, try again later
api.storageFullBase: Storage full — ppush is a free service whose file space is shared fairly.
api.storageFullFreeing: {base} {freed} will be freed within {delay} at the latest (existing files expiring): try again then, or send a smaller file.
api.storageFullInsufficient: {base} {freed} will be freed within {delay} at the latest, which will still not be enough for this file ({needed}). Try a smaller file.
api.storageFullNone: {base} No space is scheduled to be freed soon — try again later or send a smaller file ({available} available).
api.quotaFullBase: Personal quota reached ({quota} of active files per account, fair use).
api.quotaFullFreeing: {base} {freed} will be freed within {delay} at the latest (your files expiring) — or expire old file pushes from your history.
api.quotaFullNone: {base} Expire old file pushes from your history, or send a smaller file ({available} left).
api.cannotEditSelf: You cannot modify your own account here

tips.label: Tip
list: Use a unique password for every site — a breach on one shouldn't unlock the others.
A passphrase of 4–5 random words beats a short complex password — and it's easier to remember.
Length wins: every extra character makes a password exponentially harder to crack.
Never reuse your email password anywhere else — it's the master key to all your accounts.
Turn on two-factor authentication wherever you can; ppush supports it too.
Prefer a passkey over a password when offered — there's nothing to phish or leak.
A password manager remembers strong, unique passwords so you don't have to.
Sharing a password by email or chat? Send a ppush link instead — it self-destructs.
For the most sensitive secrets, set the push to expire after a single view.
The decryption key lives in the link's # fragment — it never reaches our server.
Add a passphrase to a push for a second layer only the recipient should know.
Send the link and its passphrase through two different channels.
Beware of links you didn't expect — even a ppush link can be sent to trick you.
Check the address bar: phishing pages mimic real ones with look-alike domains.
With an account you keep a history of your links and see who opened them, and when.
Free account perks: bigger files, longer expiry, more views, and view tracking.
A short expiry limits the damage if a link ends up in the wrong hands.
We only ever store ciphertext — without the link, even we can't read your secret.
Rotate important passwords periodically, and immediately after any breach.
Don't leave passwords on sticky notes; a manager or a sealed ppush note is safer.
Public Wi-Fi is fine for ppush — the secret is encrypted before it leaves your browser.
The more views you allow, the more chances a secret has to leak. Keep it low.
Avoid personal info in passwords — names, birthdays and pets are easy to guess.
Treat a recovery code like a password: store it somewhere safe and private.
Once a push is burned or expired, the content is gone for good — by design.
Generate strong passwords right here with the built-in generator.
If a site emails you your password in clear text, that's a serious red flag.
Log out on shared computers, and let the link expire once it has been read.
A secret you no longer need is a risk you no longer want — let it expire.
Trust the source before you reveal: a link from a stranger may not be a gift.
